notes.note: Recuperatores: a small panel of judges (three or five) used in the formula for damnum vi armata datum, sitting alongside the praetor; distinct from the larger standing courts.
The form of action: the panel determined how much damage the plaintiff had suffered through armed force on the defendant's part; the verdict was rendered fourfold (four times the assessed damage).
M. Terentius Varro Lucullus, brother of the more famous L. Lucullus; as praetor 76 BC he composed the formula for damnum vi armata datum, the form of action under which Pro Tullio was tried.
